props_visible: Traditional clay/ceramic mabkhara (incense burner) — handmade, textured surface with bead decoration
Traditional wooden stick display rack in background
Geometric backlit wall feature visible in background

characters_visible.wardrobe_notes: Both wearing white thobes; one has cream/beige robe overlay (bisht-style) — suggests formal wear or thobe with traditional overgarment
characters_visible.gesture_notes: One person holds the mabkhara up for examination; other reaches with right hand to examine/touch — shared moment of appreciation

notable_visual_elements: Two people examining a traditional craft object together = cultural education/appreciation moment
The mabkhara is clay/terracotta — different from the gold metal one in the previous post, showing range of traditional forms
The sharing of appreciation for a traditional object is a culturally resonant 'Saudi moment'

soft_flags.description: Two-person incense burner examination: hand dominance of object holder not definitively determinable from this angle. Right hand of examining person clearly visible. Assessed as clean given context (two-handed craft examination, not serving action).

hospitality_cues: Traditional clay mabkhara = deep heritage craft — pre-modern Saudi incense vessel
Two people examining together = culture is shared, not performed
cultural_notes.free_notes: This image shows culture being experienced, not performed for camera. Two people genuinely examining a traditional craft object together is a different and more authentic register than posed service shots. The clay mabkhara is an older form than the ornate gold version — showing the restaurant has a curated collection of traditional objects as part of the guest experience.

pattern_matches.notes: Clay mabkhara as shared cultural object — two people examining it is the hospitality story
Candid cultural moment — guests engaging with restaurant's traditional object collection
